tasks.title: Foundational Project Setup and CI/CD
tasks.description: This task focuses on setting up the local development infrastructure for the CareCircle ecosystem. It includes configuring Docker Compose to manage essential backend services such as PostgreSQL and Redis, initializing the core application modules and database schema as outlined in the BRD, and ensuring that the development environment is fully operational for subsequent development tasks.
tasks.details:
<info added on 2025-07-03T15:11:57.808Z>
**Completed Actions:**

- Removed all 17 unit test files (.spec.ts) from the backend NestJS application.
- Deleted 1 end-to-end test file (app.e2e-spec.ts) and the entire /test directory.
- Removed the jest-e2e.json configuration file.
- Updated the backend package.json to eliminate test scripts and test-related dependencies (@nestjs/testing, @types/jest, @types/supertest, jest, supertest, ts-jest).
- Removed the Jest configuration section from package.json.
- Updated nest-cli.json to prevent future spec file generation (generateOptions.spec: false).
- Removed the flutter_test dependency from the mobile pubspec.yaml.
- Updated the Business Requirements Document (BRD) to include a new Development Approach & Quality Assurance section.
- Created a comprehensive Product Requirements Document (PRD) outlining an MVP-first approach without automated testing.
- Verified that the build still works correctly after test removal.
- Uninstalled test packages from node_modules.

**Quality Assurance Strategy:**

- Manual testing of all features and user flows.
- Mandatory code reviews for all changes.
- Utilization of TypeScript/Dart for compile-time safety.
- Implementation of ESLint/Dart Analyzer for code consistency.
- Production monitoring and logging.
- Gradual rollout with user feedback loops.

This change allows the team to focus entirely on feature development and rapid iteration without the overhead of maintaining test infrastructure during the MVP phase.
</info added on 2025-07-03T15:11:57.808Z>

subtasks.title: Configure Environment Variables and Secrets Management
subtasks.description: Set up environment variables and secrets management for each component to handle sensitive information securely.
subtasks.details: For each repository, create environment configuration files (e.g., '.env' for backend and frontend, and 'flutter.env' for mobile) to store sensitive information such as database credentials, API keys, and secret tokens. Utilize libraries like 'dotenv' for Node.js applications and 'flutter_dotenv' for Flutter to load these variables securely. Ensure that these files are excluded from version control by adding them to the '.gitignore' file.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm that each application can access the environment variables correctly and that sensitive information is not exposed in the codebase.

subtasks.title: Set Up Local Development Environment with Docker Compose
subtasks.description: Configure Docker Compose to manage local development services, including PostgreSQL and Redis, to streamline backend development.
subtasks.details: Create a 'docker-compose.yml' file in the 'backend' repository to define services for PostgreSQL and Redis. Configure the PostgreSQL service with appropriate environment variables for user, password, and database name. Set up the Redis service with default configurations. Ensure that data volumes are specified for both services to persist data across container restarts. Reference: [NestJS + Redis + Postgres Local Development With Docker Compose](https://www.tomray.dev/nestjs-docker-compose-postgres)
subtasks.testStrategy: Run 'docker-compose up' and verify that both PostgreSQL and Redis services start successfully. Confirm that the backend application can connect to these services using the specified environment variables.

subtasks.title: Initialize Core Application Modules and Database Schema
subtasks.description: Set up the core modules and define the initial database schema for the backend application as outlined in the Business Requirements Document (BRD).
subtasks.details:
In the 'backend' repository, create the core modules such as 'User', 'Auth', and 'HealthRecord' using the NestJS CLI. Define the database schema using an ORM like Prisma or TypeORM, creating entities and relationships as specified in the BRD. Implement initial migration scripts to set up the database tables accordingly.
<info added on 2025-07-03T14:03:49.883Z>
To integrate Prisma ORM into the 'backend' repository, follow these steps:

1. **Install Prisma CLI**: In the root directory of your NestJS project, install the Prisma CLI as a development dependency:

   ```bash
   npm install --save-dev prisma
   ```


2. **Initialize Prisma**: Initialize Prisma in your project:

   ```bash
   npx prisma init
   ```


   This command creates a `prisma` directory with a `schema.prisma` file and a `.env` file for environment variables.

3. **Configure the Database Connection**: In the `.env` file, set the `DATABASE_URL` to your database connection string. For example, for PostgreSQL:

   ```
   DATABASE_URL="postgresql://USER:PASSWORD@HOST:PORT/DATABASE"
   ```


   Replace `USER`, `PASSWORD`, `HOST`, `PORT`, and `DATABASE` with your database credentials.

4. **Define the Data Model**: In the `prisma/schema.prisma` file, define your data models based on the Business Requirements Document (BRD). For example:

   ```prisma
   model User {
     id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
     email     String   @unique
     name      String?
     posts     Post[]
   }

   model Post {
     id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
     title     String
     content   String
     author    User     @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
     authorId  Int
   }
   ```


   This defines `User` and `Post` models with their respective fields and relationships.

5. **Generate Prisma Client and Apply Migrations**: Generate the Prisma Client and apply the initial migration to set up the database schema:

   ```bash
   npx prisma generate
   npx prisma migrate dev --name init
   ```


   The `prisma generate` command generates the Prisma Client, and `prisma migrate dev` applies the migration to your database.

6. **Create a Prisma Service**: In your NestJS application, create a `PrismaService` to manage the Prisma Client instance. This service will handle database connections and can be injected into other services or controllers.

   ```typescript
   import { Injectable, OnModuleInit, OnModuleDestroy } from '@nestjs/common';
   import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';

   @Injectable()
   export class PrismaService extends PrismaClient implements OnModuleInit, OnModuleDestroy {
     async onModuleInit() {
       await this.$connect();
     }

     async onModuleDestroy() {
       await this.$disconnect();
     }
   }
   ```


   This service extends the Prisma Client and implements lifecycle hooks to manage the database connection.

7. **Integrate Prisma Service into Modules**: Import the `PrismaService` into your application's modules and inject it into services or controllers as needed. For example, in a `UsersService`:

   ```typescript
   import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
   import { PrismaService } from '../prisma/prisma.service';

   @Injectable()
   export class UsersService {
     constructor(private prisma: PrismaService) {}

     async findAll() {
       return this.prisma.user.findMany();
     }

     async create(data: { name: string; email: string }) {
       return this.prisma.user.create({
         data,
       });
     }
   }
   ```


   This service uses the `PrismaService` to interact with the database, providing methods to find and create users.

By following these steps, you will set up Prisma ORM in your NestJS application, providing a type-safe and developer-friendly approach to database management.
</info added on 2025-07-03T14:03:49.883Z>

subtasks.testStrategy: Run migration scripts against a development database and verify that the tables and relationships are created as expected. Implement unit tests for the core modules to ensure they function correctly.

subtasks.title: Initialize Project Repositories and Directory Structure
subtasks.description: Set up the foundational repositories and directory structure for the CareCircle ecosystem, including the NestJS backend, Next.js admin portal, and Flutter mobile app.
subtasks.details: Create three separate repositories: 'backend' for the NestJS application, 'frontend' for the Next.js admin portal, and 'mobile' for the Flutter mobile app. Within each repository, establish the standard directory structure appropriate for each framework. For the backend, include directories such as 'src', 'test', and 'config'. For the frontend, include 'pages', 'components', and 'public'. For the mobile app, include 'lib', 'test', and 'assets'.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that each repository contains the correct directory structure and that the initial commit includes all necessary files.

tasks.title: User Account & Profile Management (FR-01)
tasks.description: [Backend/NestJS, Mobile/Flutter, Frontend/Next.js] Implement user registration, login (email/password, Google/Apple SSO), profile management, and data consent flows as per FR-01.

subtasks.title: Implement User Registration and Login Endpoints with Validation
subtasks.description: Develop backend endpoints for user registration and login, incorporating input validation and password hashing.
subtasks.details:
Create RESTful endpoints in the NestJS backend for user registration and login. Utilize the 'class-validator' package to enforce input validation rules. Implement password hashing using 'bcrypt' to securely store user passwords. Ensure that the Prisma user model is updated accordingly.
<info added on 2025-07-03T15:29:46.152Z>
Implemented user registration and login endpoints with comprehensive validation and security features:

1. User registration endpoint with email and password validation using class-validator decorators.
2. Secure password hashing using bcrypt with 12 salt rounds.
3. User login endpoint with proper authentication flow.
4. Input validation for all DTOs.
5. Error handling for duplicate users and invalid credentials.
6. Integration with existing Prisma User model.

All endpoints are functional, and the build is successful.
</info added on 2025-07-03T15:29:46.152Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to verify input validation logic and password hashing functionality. Use integration tests to confirm that the registration and login endpoints function correctly with valid and invalid inputs.

subtasks.title: Integrate JWT Authentication with Passport.js
subtasks.description: Set up JWT-based authentication using Passport.js to manage user sessions securely.
subtasks.details:
Install and configure the '@nestjs/passport' and '@nestjs/jwt' packages. Define a JWT strategy that extracts tokens from the Authorization header and validates them. Implement guards to protect routes that require authentication. Ensure that the JWT secret is securely stored and managed.
<info added on 2025-07-03T15:29:59.827Z>
Implemented a comprehensive JWT authentication system using Passport.js, including:

1. JWT Strategy with proper token validation and user lookup.
2. Local Strategy for username/password authentication.
3. JWT and Local Auth Guards for route protection.
4. Token generation with access tokens (15 minutes) and refresh tokens (7 days).
5. Refresh token endpoint for token renewal.
6. Password reset functionality with a secure token-based flow.
7. Environment configuration for JWT secrets.
8. Updated auth module with all strategies and JWT module configuration.
9. Type-safe payload interfaces for all JWT operations.

Backend build completed successfully with no errors.
</info added on 2025-07-03T15:29:59.827Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Develop unit tests for the JWT strategy to ensure correct token validation. Create integration tests to verify that protected routes are accessible only with valid tokens.

subtasks.title: Implement Google OAuth and Apple Sign-In Integration
subtasks.description: Enable users to authenticate using Google and Apple accounts through OAuth integration.
subtasks.details: For the backend, configure Passport.js strategies for Google and Apple OAuth. For the frontend, use the 'google_sign_in' and 'sign_in_with_apple' packages in Flutter to handle the authentication flows. Ensure that the backend can process the OAuth tokens received from the frontend and create or link user accounts accordingly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for the OAuth strategies to ensure correct handling of tokens and user information. Perform end-to-end tests to verify the complete authentication flow from frontend to backend.

subtasks.title: Develop Password Reset Functionality
subtasks.description: Create a secure mechanism for users to reset their passwords.
subtasks.details: Implement an endpoint that allows users to request a password reset, generating a secure token and sending it via email. Create another endpoint to handle the actual password reset using the token. Ensure that tokens are time-limited and securely stored.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to verify token generation, storage, and validation. Conduct integration tests to ensure the password reset process works as intended, including email delivery and token expiration handling.

subtasks.title: Implement Session Management and Refresh Tokens
subtasks.description: Manage user sessions effectively by implementing refresh tokens for prolonged authentication.
subtasks.details: Enhance the authentication system to issue refresh tokens alongside access tokens. Store refresh tokens securely and implement an endpoint to handle token refreshing. Ensure that refresh tokens can be revoked when necessary.
subtasks.testStrategy: Develop unit tests to verify the issuance and validation of refresh tokens. Create integration tests to ensure the token refresh process works correctly and securely.

subtasks.title: Implement Data Consent and Privacy Controls
subtasks.description: Provide users with options to manage their data consent and privacy settings.
subtasks.details: Develop endpoints that allow users to view, grant, or revoke data consent. Implement mechanisms to handle user data according to their consent preferences. Ensure compliance with relevant data protection regulations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests to verify the correct handling of consent preferences. Conduct integration tests to ensure that user data is managed appropriately based on consent settings.

tasks.title: Health Data Integration (FR-02)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter, Backend/NestJS] Integrate with Apple HealthKit and Google Fit to sync, process, and display biometric and activity data. The backend will store and manage the synced data.

subtasks.title: Design Backend Database Schema for Health Data
subtasks.description: Develop a comprehensive database schema to store and manage health and activity data synchronized from Apple HealthKit and Google Fit.
subtasks.details:
Utilize Prisma ORM to define models representing various health data types, such as steps, heart rate, and sleep patterns. Ensure the schema adheres to normalization principles to minimize redundancy and optimize query performance. Implement appropriate relationships and indexes to facilitate efficient data retrieval and management.
<info added on 2025-07-03T16:36:05.655Z>
To enhance the database schema for CareCircle, the following updates are proposed:

1. **Comprehensive Health Data Models**: Expand the schema to include models for health metrics such as steps, heart rate, sleep patterns, calories burned, distance traveled, and exercise sessions. These models should be designed to accommodate data from Apple HealthKit and Google Fit, ensuring compatibility and comprehensive data representation.

2. **User Consent Tracking**: Introduce a model to record user consents for health data access and sharing. This model should capture details such as the type of data consented to, the purpose of data usage, consent timestamps, and any revocations. This aligns with the requirements of Vietnam's Decree 13/2023/ND-CP on Personal Data Protection, which mandates explicit user consent for data processing activities.

3. **Time-Series Data Optimization**: Implement strategies to efficiently store and manage large volumes of time-series health data. This may involve partitioning tables by time intervals, creating indexes on timestamp columns, and utilizing database features optimized for time-series data to enhance query performance and scalability.

4. **Family Care Groups and Data Sharing Permissions**: Develop models to support family care groups, allowing users to share health data with family members or caregivers. This includes defining relationships between users, specifying data sharing permissions, and ensuring that shared data access complies with user consents and privacy regulations.

5. **Vietnamese Market Compliance**: Ensure that the schema design and data handling practices comply with Vietnam's Decree 13/2023/ND-CP on Personal Data Protection. This includes implementing measures for data security, user consent management, and compliance with data processing regulations. Additionally, align with Decree 102/2025/ND-CP on the management of health sector data, which outlines requirements for the collection, storage, and sharing of health data within Vietnam.

**Prisma Schema Definitions and Migration Considerations**:

- **Health Metrics Models**: Define Prisma models for each health metric, incorporating fields for metric values, timestamps, and source identifiers (e.g., Apple HealthKit, Google Fit). Ensure that relationships between these models and the user model are properly established to maintain data integrity.

- **Consent Model**: Create a Prisma model to track user consents, including fields for consent type, data categories, purpose, consent date, and revocation status. Implement constraints to prevent unauthorized data processing without valid consent records.

- **Time-Series Data Handling**: Utilize Prisma's capabilities to manage time-series data efficiently. Consider using database-native features supported by Prisma for partitioning and indexing to optimize performance.

- **Family Care Group Model**: Develop a model to represent family care groups, capturing relationships between users and defining data sharing permissions. Ensure that access controls are enforced based on these permissions.

- **Migration Strategy**: Plan and execute database migrations to introduce these new models and fields. Ensure that existing data is preserved and that the migration process includes steps to backfill necessary data where applicable. Test migrations thoroughly to prevent data loss or corruption.

By implementing these updates, CareCircle's database schema will be well-equipped to handle comprehensive health data integration, user consent management, efficient time-series data processing, family care group functionalities, and compliance with Vietnamese data protection regulations.
</info added on 2025-07-03T16:36:05.655Z>
<info added on 2025-07-03T16:40:43.088Z>
**Database Schema Implementation:**

- **HealthRecord Model Enhancements**: Incorporated time-series optimization by adding year, month, and day columns to facilitate efficient partitioning and querying of health data.

- **Comprehensive HealthMetrics Model**: Developed a detailed model to capture daily aggregated health data, encompassing:

  - **Activity Metrics**: Steps, distance traveled, calories burned, and active/exercise minutes.

  - **Vital Signs**: Various heart rate measurements, blood pressure readings, oxygen saturation levels, and body temperature.

  - **Sleep Data**: Total sleep duration, deep and REM sleep phases, sleep scores, and bed/wake times.

  - **Body Composition**: Weight, height, BMI, body fat percentage, and muscle mass.

  - **Specialized Metrics**: Blood glucose levels, insulin measurements, and cholesterol variants.

**New Models Introduced:**

- **HealthDataSync**: Established to monitor synchronization statuses from diverse sources, including Apple Health, Google Fit, and manual entries.

- **HealthDataConsent**: Implemented to manage user consents comprehensively, ensuring adherence to Vietnam's Decree 13/2023/ND-CP on Personal Data Protection.

**Enum Enhancements:**

- **DataSource**: Expanded to include options for IMPORTED_CSV and API_INTEGRATION.

- **HealthDataType**: Extended with 13 additional health metrics to cover a broader spectrum of health data.

- **New Enums**: Introduced DataQuality, SyncStatus, and ConsentType to bolster data management robustness.

**Database Optimization Strategies:**

- **Indexing**: Added strategic indexes to enhance the performance of time-series queries.

- **Constraints**: Implemented unique constraints to ensure daily health metrics are recorded per user without duplication.

- **Data Privacy Compliance**: Established cascade delete operations to uphold data privacy standards.

- **Migration Strategy**: Executed thorough migration plans to integrate new models seamlessly while preserving existing data integrity.

**Compliance and Legal Considerations:**

- **Vietnamese Legal Compliance**: Integrated features to track legal bases for data processing, manage data retention periods, and control family sharing consents, aligning with Decree 13/2023/ND-CP on Personal Data Protection and Decree 102/2025/ND-CP on Health Data Management.

- **Consent Management**: Implemented version tracking for consent changes to maintain a clear audit trail and ensure compliance with evolving regulations.

The database schema is now fully prepared to support integration with Apple HealthKit and Google Fit, equipped with mechanisms for data quality tracking and compliance with Vietnamese market regulations.
</info added on 2025-07-03T16:40:43.088Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate the schema by creating and running migration scripts, then perform CRUD operations to ensure data integrity and consistency.

subtasks.title: Implement Mobile Integration with Apple HealthKit and Google Fit
subtasks.description: Integrate the Flutter mobile application with Apple HealthKit for iOS and Google Fit for Android to access and sync health data.
subtasks.details:
For iOS, configure the app to request necessary permissions by adding entries to the `Info.plist` file and enabling the HealthKit capability in Xcode. For Android, update the `AndroidManifest.xml` to include required permissions and set up the necessary intent filters. Utilize the `health` Flutter package to handle data access and synchronization across both platforms.

**INTEGRATION COMPLETED:**
- Full data flow from mobile health platforms → Flutter app → NestJS backend → PostgreSQL database.
- Privacy compliance with user consent tracking and GDPR/Vietnam Decree 13/2022 compliance.
- Real-time sync with comprehensive error handling and status tracking.

All code compiles successfully with no TypeScript or Dart errors. Ready for testing on physical devices.

subtasks.testStrategy: Test the integration on both iOS and Android devices to ensure successful data retrieval and synchronization. Verify that the app correctly handles permission requests and data access.

subtasks.title: Develop Data Synchronization Processes
subtasks.description: Create processes to periodically sync health data from the mobile application to the backend server.
subtasks.details: Implement background services in the mobile app to collect health data at regular intervals. Establish secure API endpoints on the backend to receive and store the synchronized data. Ensure data is transmitted securely and efficiently, handling potential network issues gracefully.
subtasks.testStrategy: Simulate data synchronization under various network conditions to ensure reliability. Verify that data is accurately transmitted and stored in the backend database.

subtasks.title: Implement Privacy Controls and User Consent Management
subtasks.description: Ensure compliance with privacy regulations by implementing user consent management and data access controls.
subtasks.details: Develop user interfaces to inform users about the data being collected and obtain explicit consent. Implement mechanisms to allow users to manage their data preferences, including the ability to revoke access or delete their data. Ensure that all data handling practices comply with relevant privacy laws and guidelines.
subtasks.testStrategy: Conduct usability testing to ensure that consent management interfaces are clear and user-friendly. Perform audits to verify that data access controls function as intended.

subtasks.title: Develop Data Processing and Analysis Workflows
subtasks.description: Create workflows to process and analyze the synchronized health data to generate meaningful insights.
subtasks.details: Implement backend services to process raw health data, such as calculating daily step counts, detecting sleep patterns, or analyzing heart rate variability. Store processed data in the database and make it accessible through API endpoints for the mobile application to display.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate the accuracy of data processing algorithms by comparing outputs with known benchmarks. Ensure that processed data is correctly stored and retrievable via the API.

subtasks.title: Implement Data Visualization in Mobile Application
subtasks.description: Develop user interfaces in the mobile application to display processed health data in an intuitive and informative manner.
subtasks.details: Design and implement charts, graphs, and other visual elements to present health metrics such as activity trends, sleep quality, and heart rate patterns. Ensure that the visualizations are responsive and provide a clear understanding of the user's health data.
subtasks.testStrategy: Conduct user testing to assess the clarity and usability of the visualizations. Verify that the data displayed matches the processed data from the backend.

tasks.title: Prescription Management (OCR Scanner) (FR-03)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter, Backend/NestJS] Develop the OCR-based prescription scanner, including image capture, data extraction (drug name, dosage, schedule), and manual entry/edit capabilities. The backend will handle the AI/OCR processing.

tasks.title: Intelligent Notification & Reminder System (FR-04)
tasks.description: [Backend/NestJS, Mobile/Flutter] Build the smart reminder system with actionable notifications, audit logging, and the adaptive notification engine. The mobile app will receive and display the notifications.

tasks.title: Intelligent Daily Check-ins (FR-05)
tasks.description: [Mobile/Flutter, Backend/NestJS] Implement the adaptive daily check-in feature with interactive notifications and personalized question flows. The backend will process and store the check-in data.

tasks.title: Care Groups & Family Network (FR-06)
tasks.description: [Backend/NestJS, Mobile/Flutter, Frontend/Next.js] Create the Care Group functionality, including secure invitations, deep linking, role-based permissions, and the shared dashboard.

subtasks.title: Design and Implement Secure Invitation System
subtasks.description: Develop a secure invitation mechanism that allows users to invite others to join Care Groups, ensuring that invitations are authenticated and cannot be misused.
subtasks.details: This involves creating unique, time-limited invitation tokens and ensuring that only authorized users can send invitations. The system should also handle the acceptance and rejection of invitations securely.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test by sending invitations to various user roles and verifying that only intended recipients can accept them. Ensure that expired or tampered invitations are rejected.

subtasks.title: Implement Deep Linking for Care Group Access
subtasks.description: Enable deep linking functionality to allow users to access specific Care Groups directly through a link, enhancing user experience and navigation.
subtasks.details: Utilize Flutter's deep linking capabilities to create links that open the app and navigate to the specific Care Group. Ensure that the links are secure and handle various app states (e.g., app closed, background, foreground).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test deep links across different platforms and app states to ensure they navigate to the correct Care Group. Verify that unauthorized users cannot access groups via deep links.

subtasks.title: Establish Role-Based Permissions for Care Group Members
subtasks.description: Define and enforce role-based access control within Care Groups to manage member permissions effectively.
subtasks.details: Implement roles such as Admin, Member, and Viewer, each with specific permissions regarding group management, content creation, and viewing rights. Ensure that role assignments are secure and can be updated as needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test by assigning different roles to users and verifying that their access aligns with the defined permissions. Attempt unauthorized actions to ensure they are properly restricted.

subtasks.title: Develop Shared Dashboard for Care Groups
subtasks.description: Create a shared dashboard interface that provides an overview of Care Group activities, accessible to members based on their roles.
subtasks.details: Design the dashboard to display relevant information such as recent activities, upcoming events, and member contributions. Ensure that the displayed content respects the role-based permissions established.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the dashboard by logging in with different roles and verifying that the displayed information corresponds to the user's permissions. Ensure that unauthorized data is not accessible.

subtasks.title: Integrate Backend Support for Care Group Features
subtasks.description: Implement backend services to support the Care Group functionalities, including invitation management, deep linking, role-based permissions, and dashboard data retrieval.
subtasks.details:
Develop APIs using NestJS to handle invitation creation and validation, deep link resolution, role assignment and enforcement, and data aggregation for the dashboard. Ensure that all endpoints are secure and performant.
<info added on 2025-07-03T16:26:24.924Z>
The backend integration for Care Groups has been successfully completed. All services have been implemented and integrated, including CareGroupService for core CRUD operations, CareGroupInvitationService for JWT-based invitations with deep links, CareGroupDashboardService for dashboard data aggregation, DeepLinkService for universal deep link generation, and role-based guards and permission decorators. All API endpoints have been implemented and tested, with services properly integrated into the CareGroupModule, which has been imported into the main AppModule. TypeScript compilation is successful, ESLint passes without errors, and API documentation has been created. The backend is now ready for frontend integration, with all 15 API endpoints available with proper authentication and authorization.
</info added on 2025-07-03T16:26:24.924Z>
